parsed_review.summary: CP00-596 is a descriptor-only RP19.P06 client-portal implementation slice (M03 tail of 8 rows + M04 head of 2 rows). Read-only inspection via Read/Grep/Glob confirms the pack-level claim is supported. Baseline alignment is exact across closeout-pack-plan.json, docs/closeout-packs/cp00-596/manifest.json, contracts/client-portal-core-contract.json, registry pack binding, validators, and tests: pack_id CP00-596, risk_class A, unit_count 10, range RP19.P06.M03.S15-RP19.P06.M04.S02, deliverable distribution {ui:1,security_audit:4,test:4,implementation:1}, micro-title distribution {Primary Implementation Slice:8,Secondary Workflow Slice:2}, next pack CP00-597 / next subphase RP19.P06.M04.S03. All 7 required public exports exist (createClientPortalCoreCp596P06ImplementationSliceDescriptor/CaseSet in service.js; validateClientPortalCoreCp596Coverage, validateClientPortalCoreCp596P06ImplementationSliceDescriptor, createClientPortalCoreCp596HermesEvidencePacket/ClaudeReviewPacket/CloseoutHandoff in validators.js) and are surfaced through index.js. The contract-stored p06_implementation_slice_descriptor is held deep-equal to the freshly generated descriptor by scripts/validate-rp19-client-portal-core-contract.mjs, and every row carries descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, customer_safe_errors_only:true, raw_payload_included:false. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced by freezeCp596Result (all dispatch/write/audit/runtime/leak flags false; synthetic_only/no_real_data true) and the row titles are generic with no PII or real client data. The authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval:false, read_only:true, human_final_approval_required, enterprise trust not claimed from local validation; Hermes appears only as a deterministic evidence-packet descriptor with emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false (no product runtime). The manifest correctly claims production_ready:false and runtime_ready:false (status implemented_pending_review) — production_ready gates are not weakened. Review-evidence hardening passes: readiness auth-preflight succeeded with non-zero stdout and valid JSON, validation-report is within the prompt-size limit with forbidden_tools_present:false and the required read-tools source-inspection mode (summary-only prohibited due to security/audit deliverables). No P0/P1/P2 issues. One P3 informational note: the post-review artifacts referenced by the manifest (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are not yet present, which is the expected ordering for this read-only review step.

findings.title: Post-review evidence artifacts not yet present (expected pipeline ordering, non-blocking)
findings.details: The manifest lists required_pack_artifacts and evidence_refs including docs/closeout-packs/cp00-596/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but only manifest.json and command-evidence.json currently exist in that directory. Per the manifest's hardening_sequence and command-evidence post-review notes, these artifacts (the normalized review receipt produced from THIS review, plus the finding-adjudication and construction-inspection outputs) are generated downstream of this read-only review step, so they cannot be verified now. All evidence upstream of the review step was verified and is internally consistent: implementation/registry/validators/service code, contract<->generated-descriptor deep equality, plan<->manifest unit alignment, the three CP00-596 test blocks (model.test.js:921-970), command-evidence exit_code:0 set, no-write/no-real-data flags, and the read-only non-final-approver authority boundary.
findings.recommendation: Allow the downstream normalize/receipt-validate/finding-adjudication/construction-inspection steps to materialize claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, and confirm their presence and validity in the final closeout validation (npm run closeout-pack:validate) before commit. No change required to the implementation under review.
